title: Use result of a formula column as a trigger for automation
excerpt:
I would like to be able to make a calculation using a formula, and when the result is higher than a certain amount, to be able to trigger an automation that creates an item on another board. 
For example, my event costs …

posts.author: nathanru (Nathan)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would like to be able to make a calculation using a formula, and when the result is higher than a certain amount, to be able to trigger an automation that creates an item on another board.

For example, my event costs 150.000 and I have 100 participants. I would like that if 150000/100 is bigger than 1000, then it should open an item on another board for special budget approvals.

posts.author: MRad (M Rad)
posts.cookedHtml: This is essential and Monday’s competitors have this feature. A real example of how unsophisticated Monday’s software is.

posts.author: Mykola (Mykola Pustovoychenko)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @nathanru

Indeed, it is a common problem in monday.com
We at resynced.io have a temporary workaround for this problem until it is implemented natively in monday.com

Please find a short video about how you can do that with by using Google Sheets formula: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=f51NaLaeT3I

posts.author: mjp (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
100% basic functionality that’s required.

My use cases: I have request forms with multiple scoring dimensions (e.g. # of customers, # of teams, systems, products) that are entered by drop-down / multiple choice / number entry… and a formula column that converts these dimensions into a normalized score.

Now, say if that score is less than 3, do nothing; if it’s 4-7, send a notification to one person; if it’s 8-10 send a notification to the requester and 3 other people.

can I build this without using the formula? Yes, I COULD. It would be ridiculously complicated to do so - many many multiple nested IF statements would need to be built to cover the possibilities, vs a very simple statement if we’re using the normalized score.

It seems like this functionality was asked for 6 years ago, and we’re still waiting

posts.author: shannon-cxmonday (Shannon Thao)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Nathan and Mike! Shannon here with the monday team. We left a new update in the Feature Request thread, below, to provide everyone with an update on the Formula Column and automations.

Include formula columns in automation recipes Feature requests
Hi everyone! We know many of you have been eagerly awaiting an update here, and we really appreciate your patience on this. I’m excited to share where things stand with formula column support in automations and workflows. What’s live now: You can now use formula column values as dynamic text in automation actions like Notify, Send Email, and Create Update. This means calculated values can now be part of your automated communication and updates. What’s still on the way: Formula columns can’…

We really appreciate you both for sticking this out with us! This feature is on the way and we’ll continue to keep everyone updated in the linked thread. Thank you for your continued feedback.

posts.author: sg123 (Scott G)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @nathanru, hope your good. I completely agree with you and something that should be addressed which is why we have created the Community Cookbook. Its a library of triggers and actions that is constantly growing. (currently in review at the time of this post) but we would love to hear your thoughts.

Here is the threshold Formula column trigger we have created: Formula Column Threshold Trigger — Custom Trigger for monday.com | Community Cookbook

Here is all the recipes currently in the Cookbook Recipes — Custom Automation Blocks for monday.com | Community Cookbook

If you have any other ideas aswell here is where the community are posting ideas and we are voting for the next builds. Idea Hub — Suggest Automation Recipes | Community Cookbook

Let me know your thoughts we would love to hear more about what other problems your having.
